//: Registry of terminal emulators the harness (run_bench.py) knows how to launch. `mode` is one of: 'direct' (run the binary on the workload file directly, no shell), 'pty' (spawn under a harness-owned PTY as controlling terminal — needed for programs that require a real tty on their own stdin/stdout, like rusty_term's TUI/passthrough mode), or 'subprocess' (plain child process — used for windowed/GUI terminals, which open their own display connection and don't care about inherited stdio). `{cmd}` in `args` is replaced with the shell command to run inside. Edit or add entries freely; unknown/uninstalled binaries are skipped at run time, not treated as errors.

terminals.label: cat (no emulation — floor reference)
terminals.notes: Not a terminal. Shows the raw pipe-drain floor with zero VT parsing/rendering, for scale against everything else.

terminals.label: rusty_term (TUI/passthrough)
terminals.notes: Build with `cargo build --release` (target/release/rusty_term is found automatically; override with $RUSTY_TERM_BIN). Runs attached to a PTY the harness allocates, since raw-mode setup needs a real controlling tty on stdin.

terminals.label: Ghostty
terminals.notes: https://ghostty.org — the -e flag consumes the rest of argv as the command to run; check `ghostty --help` if this changes across versions.

terminals.label: foot
terminals.notes: Wayland-only — needs a Wayland compositor; Xvfb (X11) won't help here.

terminals.label: Konsole
terminals.notes: --nofork keeps the process attached so the harness can time it; without it Konsole forks to a background instance and returns immediately.
